nodes.name: China
nodes.role: Dominant in extraction and especially processing/refining; ~70% cobalt refining, ~90%+ REE processing, ~99% low-purity gallium (2024, USGS), ~68% germanium, ~90% graphite processing, ~95% manganese refining
nodes.regulatory: Export Control Law (2020); phased controls: gallium/germanium licensing (Aug 2023), graphite (Oct/Dec 2023), REE processing-tech ban (Dec 2023), antimony (Sep 2024), Dec 2024 ban on Ga/Ge/Sb to US, tungsten/tellurium/bismuth/molybdenum/indium (Feb 2025), 7 medium/heavy REEs incl Dy/Tb (Apr 2025), Oct 2025 extraterritorial FDPR-style rules (Announcements 55-62); most Oct 2025 measures suspended Nov 2025-Nov 2026 after Trump-Xi truce
nodes.reserves: World's largest REE reserves (~44Mt); ~35Mt+ REO at Bayan Obo

nodes.name: United States
nodes.role: Major end-user; import-reliant on 24 of 50 critical minerals from China; building domestic REE magnet chain via DoD
nodes.regulatory: IRA EV-credit sourcing rules; DoD equity stakes (MP Materials); Defense Production Act

nodes.name: European Union
nodes.regulatory: Critical Raw Materials Act (2024): strategic project list and 2030 benchmarks

nodes.name: Australia
nodes.role: Leading lithium (spodumene) and REE miner outside China; >50% of mined lithium

nodes.name: Democratic Republic of Congo
nodes.role: ~76% of global mined cobalt; major copper
nodes.regulatory: ARECOMS cobalt export quota (Oct 2025), ~96,600 t/yr cap from 2026; 10% royalty; Gécamines state miner

nodes.name: Indonesia
nodes.role: World's largest nickel producer/refiner; ~2nd cobalt via HPAL
nodes.regulatory: 2020 ore export ban drove downstream buildout (nickel export value ~$6B 2013 to ~$30B 2022); Chinese-dominated smelting

nodes.name: Chile
nodes.regulatory: National Lithium Strategy; Codelco-SQM JV (Nova Andino Litio) giving state 51%+ of Atacama from 2025
nodes.reserves: Largest copper reserves; major Atacama lithium

nodes.name: Philippines
nodes.role: World's 2nd-largest nickel ore producer/supplier; ~10.5% of global 2024; overwhelmingly exports raw ore to China
nodes.regulatory: Mining Act 1995 (RA 7942); MPSA/FTAA via DENR-MGB; proposed raw-ore export ban passed Senate Feb 2025 but ban provision removed June 2025; 4% excise + royalties

nodes.name: Japan
nodes.role: End-user (battery/electronics); strategic upstream investor via Sumitomo, Mitsui, Sojitz, Pacific Metals

nodes.name: South Korea
nodes.role: Major battery/cathode maker (LG, Samsung SDI, SK On, POSCO)

nodes.name: Myanmar
nodes.role: World's ~3rd largest REE producer (~31kt 2024, up from ~12kt 2022); ~two-thirds of China's heavy REE (Dy/Tb) imports by volume 2017-2024; Kachin ionic-clay mines KIA-controlled
nodes.regulatory: KIA imposes 35,000 yuan/t + 20% levy on REE concentrate exports; largely unregulated post-2021 coup

nodes.name: Canada
nodes.role: Nickel, cobalt, germanium (Teck refines Red Dog zinc concentrate)

nodes.name: Brazil
nodes.role: Niobium, nickel (Vale), iron ore; emerging REE

nodes.name: MP Materials
nodes.position: Only scaled US REE miner (Mountain Pass); building 10X magnet plant in Texas
nodes.ownership: NYSE:MP; DoD becoming largest shareholder via $400M Series A preferred + up to $350M more; Shenghe retains a minority stake
nodes.subsidiaries: MP Mine Operations LLC

nodes.name: Glencore
nodes.subsidiaries: Kamoto Copper Company (KCC), Mutanda Mining (DRC); Murrin Murrin (Australia)

nodes.name: CATL (Contemporary Amperex Technology)
nodes.subsidiaries: Brunp (recycling); stakes in CMOC, Pilbara, QMB Indonesia HPAL

nodes.name: Tianqi Lithium
nodes.ownership: SZSE:002466, HKEX:9696; ~22.16% of SQM (from 23.77% acquired 2018, trimmed to ~22% in 2025-26); 51% of TLEA JV with IGO
nodes.subsidiaries: TLEA; Kwinana refinery

nodes.name: Lynas Rare Earths
nodes.position: Largest producer of separated REE outside China
nodes.subsidiaries: Lynas Malaysia (Kuantan); Kalgoorlie facility; Mount Weld mine

nodes.name: Albemarle
nodes.ownership: NYSE:ALB; 49% of Talison/Greenbushes; 60% of MARBL JV (Wodgina)
nodes.subsidiaries: La Negra (Chile), Kemerton (WA), Salar de Atacama

nodes.name: SQM (Sociedad Química y Minera)
nodes.ownership: NYSE:SQM; Tianqi ~22%; Codelco JV (Nova Andino Litio) giving state 51%+
nodes.subsidiaries: Salar de Atacama brine, Antofagasta plants

nodes.name: Shenghe Resources
nodes.position: Chinese state-adjacent REE trader; historically processed Mountain Pass concentrate
nodes.subsidiaries: Shenghe Resources (Singapore) — MP offtake counterparty

nodes.name: Vale
nodes.subsidiaries: Vale Base Metals; PT Vale Indonesia (44.3%)

nodes.name: Tsingshan Holding Group
nodes.position: World's largest nickel/stainless producer; dominant in Indonesia
nodes.subsidiaries: Shanghai Decent; IMIP Morowali; PT Weda Bay Nickel (51.3%)

nodes.name: CMOC Group (China Molybdenum)
nodes.subsidiaries: Tenke Fungurume (80%), Kisanfu/KFM (DRC); IXM trading arm

nodes.name: Zhejiang Huayou Cobalt
nodes.position: China's 2nd largest cobalt producer; vertically integrated
nodes.subsidiaries: Bamo (cathode); Huafei/Huayue Indonesia HPAL; DRC mines

nodes.name: GEM Co.
nodes.subsidiaries: QMB New Materials (Indonesia HPAL with Tsingshan, CATL, Hanwa)

nodes.name: Ganfeng Lithium
nodes.subsidiaries: Stakes in Cauchari-Olaroz (Argentina), Mount Marion (Australia), Mali

nodes.name: China Northern Rare Earth
nodes.position: World's largest REE producer by volume
nodes.subsidiaries: Baotou processing; processes Bayan Obo concentrate

nodes.name: Zijin Mining
nodes.ownership: SHA:601899, HKEX:2899; 39.6% of Kamoa-Kakula + 13.59% of Ivanhoe (44.45% economic)

nodes.name: Freeport-McMoRan
nodes.subsidiaries: Morenci, Cerro Verde, El Abra, PT-FI

nodes.name: Rio Tinto
nodes.minerals: Copper, iron ore, aluminium, lithium, TiO2, borates
nodes.ownership: ASX/LSE/NYSE:RIO; 30% of Escondida; 66% Oyu Tolgoi; acquired Arcadium Lithium

nodes.name: BHP
nodes.position: World's largest miner by value; ~1.86Mt copper 2025

nodes.name: Anglo American
nodes.position: Major diversified miner; copper in Chile/Peru

nodes.name: IGO Limited
nodes.position: Australian lithium/nickel; JV partner in Greenbushes
nodes.ownership: ASX:IGO; 49% of TLEA JV with Tianqi

nodes.name: Nvidia
nodes.position: Leading AI chip designer; TSMC customer

nodes.name: Tesla
nodes.position: Leading Western EV maker; vertical battery integration

nodes.name: Ford
nodes.position: Legacy automaker; nickel offtake via Pomalaa

nodes.name: Eurasian Resources Group
nodes.position: Metalkol RTR tailings reprocessing in DRC

nodes.name: Jinchuan Group
nodes.position: China's largest cobalt refiner; ~20% global cobalt demand

nodes.name: Eramet
nodes.position: Diversified French miner; Weda Bay partner

nodes.name: Sumitomo Metal Mining
nodes.position: Major Japanese miner/refiner; Philippine HPAL operator
nodes.ownership: TSE:5713; 26.04% of Nickel Asia; 84.375%→100% of Coral Bay (CBNC); 62.5%-75% of Taganito HPAL

nodes.name: Nickel Asia Corporation
nodes.position: Philippines' largest nickel producer; PNIA ~85% of PH output
nodes.ownership: PSE:NIKL; Sumitomo Metal Mining Philippine Holdings 26.04%
nodes.subsidiaries: Taganito Mining (65%), Rio Tuba (60%), Cagdianao Mining, Hinatuan Mining, Dinapigue

nodes.name: Global Ferronickel Holdings (FNI)
nodes.position: Philippines' 2nd-largest nickel ore exporter; 5.448M WMT shipped 2024; ~93% of 2024 revenue from China
nodes.ownership: PSE:FNI; principal subsidiary Platinum Group Metals Corp (PGMC); 20% of Guangdong Century Tsingshan ferronickel smelter (China)
nodes.subsidiaries: PGMC (Cagdianao, Surigao), Ipilan Nickel (Palawan)

nodes.name: SR Metals
nodes.position: Mid-tier PH nickel; ~10.09Bn kg ore to China 2020-2024 (~$195M)
nodes.subsidiaries: Tubay project (Agusan del Norte); Dinagat via Vista Buena

nodes.name: Marcventures Holdings
nodes.position: Mid-tier PH nickel; Surigao del Sur; main market China

nodes.name: OceanaGold
nodes.subsidiaries: Didipio Mine

nodes.name: Sagittarius Mines (SMI)
nodes.position: Developer of Tampakan, SE Asia's largest undeveloped Cu-Au deposit
nodes.ownership: Tampakan Group of Companies (Salvador Zamora); Glencore exited 2015; Indophil historically

nodes.name: PT Vale Indonesia
nodes.ownership: IDX:INCO; Vale Canada 44.3%, MIND ID 20%, Sumitomo Metal Mining 15%, others 20.7%

nodes.name: MIND ID (Inalum)
nodes.ownership: State-owned; 51.24% PT Freeport Indonesia, 20% PT Vale Indonesia

nodes.name: PT Antam
nodes.position: Indonesian state miner; CATL JV partner
nodes.ownership: IDX:ANTM (MIND ID group); 10% PT Weda Bay Nickel

nodes.name: Codelco
nodes.ownership: Chilean state-owned; 51%+ of Nova Andino Litio JV with SQM

nodes.name: Rare Earth Elements
nodes.uses: Permanent magnets (NdFeB) for EV motors, wind turbines; electronics, defense
nodes.criticality: US & EU critical; extreme supply concentration

nodes.name: Neodymium-Praseodymium (NdPr)
nodes.uses: NdFeB magnets (EV traction motors, wind turbines)
nodes.top_producers: China (Bayan Obo), Australia (Mount Weld), US (Mountain Pass)

nodes.name: Dysprosium/Terbium (heavy REE)
nodes.criticality: Heavy REE; Apr 2025 China export controls

nodes.name: Gallium
nodes.concentration: China dominant; Aug 2023 licensing, Dec 2024 US ban, Jan 2025 tech controls

nodes.name: Germanium
nodes.uses: Fiber optics, infrared/night-vision optics, solar cells

nodes.name: Antimony
nodes.uses: Flame retardants, ammunition, night vision, batteries
nodes.concentration: Sep 2024 controls, Dec 2024 US ban; exports fell ~97%, prices ~tripled

nodes.name: Silicon / Polysilicon
nodes.criticality: EU strategic; foundational to Pax-Silica framing

nodes.name: Greenbushes
nodes.size: World's largest hard-rock lithium mine; ~1.5Mtpa spodumene capacity; >35yr mine life
nodes.operators: Talison Lithium Australia: Albemarle 49%, TLEA 51% (Tianqi 26.01% + IGO 24.99% indirect)

nodes.name: Mountain Pass
nodes.size: Only scaled US REE mine
nodes.operators: MP Materials
nodes.production: Leading US NdPr concentrate; building domestic separation

nodes.name: Bayan Obo
nodes.size: World's largest REE deposit; ~35Mt+ REO (~80%+ China reserves, ~36% world)
nodes.operators: Mined by Baotou Steel Union (Baogang); processed by China Northern Rare Earth
nodes.production: Dominant global light-REE (NdPr) source; REE co-product of iron ore

nodes.name: Escondida
nodes.size: World's largest copper mine; ~1.35Mtpa capacity
nodes.operators: BHP 57.5% (operator), Rio Tinto 30%, JECO 12.5%

nodes.name: Grasberg
nodes.size: One of world's largest Cu-Au mines; ~1.62Bt ore reserve (2022)
nodes.operators: PT Freeport Indonesia: MIND ID 51.24%, Freeport-McMoRan 48.8%
nodes.production: Disrupted by Sept 2025 fatal Block Cave incident; ~600kt Cu loss expected through 2026

nodes.name: Kamoa-Kakula
nodes.size: Africa's largest, world's 3rd largest copper mine; >600ktpa capacity
nodes.operators: Ivanhoe 39.6%, Zijin 39.6%, Crystal River 0.8%, DRC govt 20%

nodes.name: Tenke Fungurume (TFM)
nodes.size: One of two largest DRC Cu-Co mines; ~37kt Co capacity
nodes.operators: CMOC 80%, Gécamines 20%

nodes.name: Kisanfu (KFM)
nodes.size: World's largest cobalt mine by output; ~50kt Co capacity
nodes.operators: CMOC (majority) with Gécamines; CATL held prior interest
nodes.production: Major driver of CMOC 2024 record output

nodes.name: Mutanda
nodes.size: Large Glencore Cu-Co mine (restarted 2021)
nodes.operators: Glencore
nodes.production: Glencore ~3,925t combined (with KCC) Q4 2025 DRC export quota

nodes.name: Weda Bay
nodes.size: Major nickel laterite; feeds IWIP industrial park
nodes.operators: PT Weda Bay Nickel: Tsingshan 51.3%, Eramet 38.7%, PT Antam 10%
nodes.production: One of world's largest nickel mines

nodes.name: Morowali (IMIP)
nodes.size: Indonesia Morowali Industrial Park; integrated nickel/HPAL hub
nodes.operators: Tsingshan-led; hosts Huayue, QMB HPAL JVs

nodes.name: Sorowako
nodes.size: PT Vale's flagship nickel operation, South Sulawesi
nodes.operators: PT Vale Indonesia; HPAL JV with Huayou (Sorowako, Pomalaa)

nodes.name: Salar de Atacama
nodes.size: World's largest lithium brine reserves (CORFO-owned)
nodes.operators: SQM (~65% of Chile output) & Albemarle (~35%); Codelco taking control from 2025

nodes.name: Pilgangoora
nodes.size: ~223Mt @ 1.27% Li2O; one of world's largest hard-rock lithium mines
nodes.operators: Pilbara Minerals

nodes.name: Mount Weld
nodes.size: 106.6Mt @ 4.12% TREO resource; 32Mt @ 6.44% TREO reserve (Aug 2024); largest REE outside China
nodes.operators: Lynas Rare Earths
nodes.production: Feeds Kalgoorlie + Malaysia; ~7,200tpa NdPr expanding to 12,000tpa

nodes.name: Kachin/Kayah REE district
nodes.size: Major ionic-clay heavy REE; ~370+ mining sites by end-2024
nodes.operators: KIA-controlled; sold to Chinese processors

nodes.name: Coral Bay HPAL (CBNC)
nodes.size: First PH HPAL; 24,000t/yr Ni + 2,500t/yr Co, Rio Tuba, Palawan
nodes.operators: Sumitomo Metal Mining (moving to 100% after buying NAC's 15.625%)
nodes.production: Ni/Co mixed sulfide refined in Japan

nodes.name: Taganito HPAL
nodes.size: ~30,000-36,000t/yr Ni + ~3,000t/yr Co; Claver, Surigao del Norte
nodes.operators: Sumitomo Metal Mining 62.5%-75%, Nickel Asia ~10-22.5%, Mitsui 15%

nodes.name: Taganito Mine
nodes.size: Largest PH nickel mine; ~70kt Ni content 2023
nodes.operators: Taganito Mining Corp (Nickel Asia 65%, Pacific Metals 33.5%, Sojitz 1.5%)

nodes.name: Rio Tuba Mine
nodes.size: Nickel laterite, Bataraza, Palawan
nodes.operators: Rio Tuba Nickel Mining Corp (Nickel Asia 60%)
nodes.production: Saprolite/limonite export + feed to Coral Bay HPAL

nodes.name: Tampakan
nodes.size: ~2.94Bt @ 0.51% Cu, 0.19g/t Au = 15Mt Cu + 17.6Moz Au; SE Asia's largest undeveloped Cu-Au
nodes.operators: Sagittarius Mines (Tampakan Group / Zamora)
nodes.production: Undeveloped; targeting ~375ktpa Cu + 360koz Au over 17yr; ~2028 start

nodes.name: Didipio
nodes.size: Cu-Au porphyry, Nueva Vizcaya/Quirino; ~6.2Moz Au reserves company-wide 2024
nodes.operators: OceanaGold Philippines (OGPI) 80% OceanaGold / 20% public (FTAA No. 001)

nodes.name: Cagdianao Mine (PGMC)
nodes.size: MPSA 007-92-X, ~5,220 ha, >110Mt WMT resource @ ~1.2% Ni; ~7.6M WMT/yr capacity
nodes.operators: Platinum Group Metals Corp (Global Ferronickel)

nodes.name: Zambales nickel district
nodes.size: Luzon laterite/chromite district (Sta. Cruz-Candelaria)
nodes.operators: DMCI (ZDMC/ZCMC), Eramen Minerals, BenguetCorp Nickel Mines
nodes.production: Individual mines ~0.6-1.5M WMT/yr; exports to Japan/Korea

nodes.name: Surigao/Dinagat nickel district
nodes.size: Mindanao's dominant, highest-grade nickel laterite region (Surigao Mineral Reservation)
nodes.operators: Nickel Asia (Taganito, Cagdianao, Hinatuan), FNI/PGMC, SR Metals, Marcventures nearby

links.description: MP Materials owns and operates Mountain Pass, the only scaled US REE mine
Historic offtake (Mar 4 2022 via Shenghe Singapore) to process Mountain Pass concentrate in China; shipments to China ceased Q3 2025; agreement expires Jan 2026, not renewed
Shenghe retains a minority equity stake in MP Materials (undisclosed %; governance complexity)
DoD $400M Series A preferred (largest shareholder) + up to $350M more + $150M loan + $110/kg NdPr price floor, announced Jul 10 2025
DoD 10-yr offtake of 100% of 10X facility magnet output; >=$140M annual EBITDA threshold
Produces NdPr concentrate/oxide; building magnet capacity
Albemarle owns 49% of Talison Lithium Australia (Greenbushes)
Tianqi holds 26.01% indirect interest via TLEA/Talison
IGO holds 24.99% indirect interest via TLEA
TLEA JV: Tianqi 51% / IGO 49%; holds 51% of Talison and 100% of Kwinana refinery
Tianqi holds ~22.16% of SQM (acquired 23.77% for $4.1B in 2018; trimmed toward ~22% 2025-26); antitrust limits board influence
Codelco-SQM JV Nova Andino Litio: Codelco 51%+share; state gets 85% of operating margin from 2031; formed Dec 2025
SQM operates Salar de Atacama brine (~65% of Chile output) under CORFO lease
Albemarle operates Salar de Atacama brine (~35% of Chile output) under CORFO lease
ARECOMS quota: CMOC ~6,650t Q4 2025; annual cap ~96,600t from 2026
CMOC majority owner; world's largest cobalt mine by output
World's largest cobalt miner: 114,165t 2024 (~41% global share, per Benchmark/Cobalt Institute)
DRC cobalt hydroxide → Chinese intermediaries/refiners → battery cathode chains
CMOC/IXM cobalt & DRC material feeds Chinese refiners supplying CATL
Huayou supplies cathode precursor (pCAM) to CATL
CATL has held cobalt-access stakes/relationships with CMOC (and prior Kisanfu interest)
Sources lithium (Pilbara stake, Bolivia, Millennial); LFP/NMC cells
Vale Canada 44.3% of PT Vale Indonesia
Sumitomo Metal Mining 15% of PT Vale Indonesia
MIND ID 20% of PT Vale Indonesia
Pomalaa & Sorowako HPAL; Vale option up to 30% of Pomalaa (Huayou 53%, Ford 17%, Vale 30%)
Pomalaa Block HPAL three-way: Huayou ~53%, Ford ~17%, PT Vale up to 30%; ~120ktpa MHP
Ford secures MHP/nickel from Pomalaa for EV batteries
Tsingshan 51.3% of PT Weda Bay Nickel
Eramet 38.7% of PT Weda Bay Nickel
PT Antam 10% of PT Weda Bay Nickel
Tsingshan leads IMIP Morowali industrial park
Huayue HPAL (Huayou, Tsingshan, CMOC) at Morowali
QMB New Materials HPAL (Tsingshan, GEM, CATL, Hanwa)
CMOC holds stake in Huayue HPAL JV
2020 ore export ban forced downstream investment; nickel export value ~$6B (2013)→~$30B (2022)
Aug 2023 licensing; Dec 2024 US ban; Jan 2025 extraction-tech controls; ~99% global primary low-purity gallium (2024, USGS)
Oct/Dec 2023 export licensing for synthetic + flake graphite
Dec 2023 processing-tech ban; Apr 2025 controls on 7 medium/heavy REEs; Oct 2025 extraterritorial rules (suspended Nov 2025)
Sep 2024 controls; Dec 2024 US ban; exports fell ~97%, prices ~tripled
Apr 2025 export controls on Dy/Tb and 5 other medium/heavy REEs
Feb 2025 export controls (with tellurium, bismuth, molybdenum, indium)
Jul 2025 export licensing on lithium extraction/battery cathode tech
US semiconductor export controls vs China critical-mineral controls; Nov 2025 truce suspended some measures to Nov 2026
IRA EV-credit sourcing rules; Treasury eased graphite until 2027
Critical Raw Materials Act 2024 strategic list & 2030 benchmarks
World's largest REE source; NdPr dominant
Processes Bayan Obo concentrate (mined by Baotou Steel Union); ~37% held by Baogang
World's largest REE producer by volume (light REE)
State-owned Baogang Group controls Bayan Obo
Heavy REE (Dy/Tb) reserves up 92% in 2024 update
Largest separated-REE producer outside China; ~7,200tpa NdPr expanding to 12,000tpa
US DoD contracts for Lynas US heavy-REE processing facility (Texas)
Kachin REE concentrate exported to China (Yunnan); KIA 35,000 yuan/t + 20% levy
MIND ID 51.24% of PT Freeport Indonesia
Zijin 39.6% direct (44.45% economic incl. 13.59% Ivanhoe stake)
China's largest cobalt refiner; ~20% global demand
Sumitomo Metal Mining Philippine Holdings 26.04% of Nickel Asia
Taganito Mining Corp: NAC 65%, Pacific Metals 33.5%, Sojitz 1.5%
Sumitomo Metal Mining 62.5%-75% of Taganito HPAL
NAC divesting 15.625% to Sumitomo; exiting HPAL business
Largest PH nickel mine ~70kt Ni content 2023
Ni/Co mixed sulfide shipped to Japan for refining (SMM)
MSP to SMM's IRA-relevant nickel sulfate in Japan
2nd-largest PH nickel ore exporter; 5.448M WMT shipped 2024 (+15.5%)
~93% of FNI 2024 revenue from China; 7% Indonesia
FNI holds 20% of Guangdong Century Tsingshan ferronickel smelter (China)
Nickel laterite ore; ~61% of FNI 2024 shipment revenue
Tubay (Agusan del Norte) nickel laterite; ~10.09Bn kg to China 2020-2024
Surigao del Sur nickel laterite (MMDC, MPSA 016-93-XIII)
~35Mt of 44.97Mt WMT 2024 ore exports to China (>90% of China's imports)
MPSA/FTAA via DENR-MGB; proposed ore export ban (Senate Feb 2025) removed June 2025
SMI (Tampakan Group/Zamora) develops Tampakan; Glencore exited 2015
FTAA + open-pit ban lifted 2021; targeting 2028 start
Fabs consume gallium (GaN/GaAs) exposed to China controls
ASML supplies EUV lithography to TSMC
Panasonic supplies battery cells to Tesla
LFP battery lithium sourcing; vertical integration
Teck refines germanium from Red Dog zinc concentrate
